text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Meadowbrook lived an elderly owl named Olive. Olive was famous amongst the locals not just for her wisdom but for her extraordinary memory. One sunny afternoon, after a delightful dinner of worms caught with her own claws, Olive lazily perched atop her favorite ancient oak tree.

As she dozed off, she felt a peculiar sensation.

"Woken-up owl?" Olive murmured dreamily. "What's on my mind?"

Taking a deep breath, Olive squawked quietly.

"A feather... from last autumn."

Her mind snapped back to reality. There it was, just as she remembered—a dark plum feather she'd dropped.

Suddenly curious, she decided to investigate. Gathering her feathers tightly against her skin, she tilted sideways.

That morning, while visiting Mr. Tweedy's garden, Olive had noticed a peculiar quetztal feather. It was unique—a black and white stripe that seemed enchanted.
